AnswerMetadata.description: Diagnostic metadata for an answer call.

llm_model.description: OpenRouter model identifier that produced the answer. Format: ``vendor/model``, e.g. ``anthropic/claude-haiku-4.5``.
llm_model.title: Llm Model

llm_tokens_out.description: Output tokens generated (the answer text itself).
llm_tokens_out.title: Llm Tokens Out

pipeline_version.description: Version label of the retrieval pipeline that produced the sources, copied from :class:`PipelineMetadata.version` (e.g. ``dharma_v2-rerank0-parents1-pali1``). Lets a client correlate answer quality with the retrieval config.
pipeline_version.title: Pipeline Version

retrieval_metadata.description: Full :class:`PipelineMetadata` from the retrieval call, embedded so consumers don't need a second round-trip to /api/query for diagnostics.

style.description: Effective answer style applied to this request (``auto`` / ``concise`` / ``detailed``). Resolved from the request override, else from the server-side default.
style.title: Style

trace_id.description: UUID4 identifying this single request/response. Generated server-side and propagated through structlog context, Phoenix spans, and the response payload. Used as the primary key for ``POST /api/feedback`` so a client can attach 👍/👎 to a specific answer.
trace_id.title: Trace Id

AnswerMetadata.title: AnswerMetadata

expand_definitional.description: Forwarded to retrieval. Override definitional query expansion (rag-day-28). ``None`` defers to server default.
expand_definitional.title: Expand Definitional

expand_pali.description: Forwarded to the underlying retrieval call. ``None`` defers to the server-side ``glossary_expand_pali_default``. ``True``/``False`` overrides per request — useful for side-by-side debugging on the same question.
expand_pali.title: Expand Pali

forbidden_works.description: Forwarded to retrieval. Drops sources whose ``work_canonical_id`` appears in this list before they reach the LLM.
forbidden_works.title: Forbidden Works

foundational_boost.description: Forwarded to retrieval. Override foundational mapping boost (rag-day-28). ``None`` defers to server default.
foundational_boost.title: Foundational Boost

model.description: Optional override of the OpenRouter model id (e.g. ``anthropic/claude-haiku-4.5``). ``None`` uses the server-side ``answer_llm_model`` setting. Useful for A/B comparing models without restarting the service.
model.title: Model

style.description: Length/depth preference. ``None`` defers to server-side ``answer_default_style`` (default ``'auto'``). Override per-request when the client UI exposes a concise/detailed toggle.
style.title: Style

top_k.description: Number of source passages to retrieve and feed to the LLM. Tighter cap than /api/query (which allows 20) because each extra passage costs ~500-1500 input tokens — 5 parents fit comfortably in any context window without inflating the per-request bill.
top_k.title: Top K

AnswerResponse.description: Body of the response from POST /api/answer.

answer.description: Synthesised answer in the language of the question. Inline citations appear as ``[work_id]``, e.g. ``[mn36]`` or ``[sn56.11]``. Empty string is returned when retrieval produced no sources — consumers should display a fallback message in that case.
answer.title: Answer

citations.description: Distinct ``work_canonical_id`` strings that the LLM actually cited (subset of ``sources``). Extracted by matching ``[work_id]`` patterns in the answer text. Empty list when the LLM declined to cite (e.g. 'sources do not answer the question').
citations.title: Citations

llm_latency_ms.description: Time spent in the LLM call (network + generation).
llm_latency_ms.title: Llm Latency Ms

retrieval_latency_ms.description: Time spent in the retrieval pipeline.
retrieval_latency_ms.title: Retrieval Latency Ms

sources.description: The exact sources fed to the LLM, in the same order. Useful for rendering ``[work_id]`` citation chips in the UI back to the corresponding passage.
sources.title: Sources

AnswerResponse.title: AnswerResponse

AnswerSnapshot.description:
Fields of the rated answer that we persist alongside the vote.

All fields are required so review through ``psql`` doesn't need a
join — see ``docs/concepts/23-feedback-widget.md`` section "Схема
таблицы". Mirrors the subset of ``AnswerMetadata`` + ``query`` /
``answer`` text that the frontend already has after a successful
answer.
AnswerSnapshot.title: AnswerSnapshot

answer_snapshot.description: Echo of the rated answer's metadata so the row is self-contained (no audit_log join). Removed once audit_log exists (app-day-49).

comment.description: Optional free-text comment (≤2000 chars). Guards against megabyte-sized rants without restricting genuine feedback.
comment.title: Comment

trace_id.description: UUID4 of the answer being rated. Comes from ``AnswerMetadata.trace_id`` in the prior /api/answer or /api/answer/stream response.
trace_id.title: Trace Id

FeedbackResponse.description: Body of the response from POST /api/feedback.

saved.description: Always ``true`` on a 2xx — operation is idempotent (upsert). Reserved as a non-trivial field so the response stays a JSON object even after future additions (e.g. ``echo_id``).
saved.title: Saved

FeedbackResponse.title: FeedbackResponse

PipelineMetadata.description:
Diagnostic metadata about which pipeline produced the response.

Embedded so a downstream consumer can reason about answer quality
when the server-side defaults change. Stable in the sense that the
*fields* are guaranteed; *values* are free to evolve as we tune.

collection.description: Qdrant collection that served the query.
collection.title: Collection

expand_definitional.description: Whether the definitional template (rag-day-28) actually rewrote the query. ``True`` only when the regexp matched and a longer gloss was substituted — distinct from the toggle being on but no pattern matching.
expand_definitional.title: Expand Definitional

expand_pali.description: Whether the query was rewritten via the Pāli glossary (rag-day-23) before encoding. ``False`` when either disabled by setting/request or when no glossary terms matched the query — the metadata field tracks the *effective* expansion, not just the toggle.
expand_pali.title: Expand Pali

foundational_boost.description: Whether the foundational mapping (rag-day-28) actually boosted at least one work for this query. ``True`` only when a curated term/alias was found and its work appeared in the candidate pool.
foundational_boost.title: Foundational Boost

n_candidates.description: RRF candidate pool size before truncation to ``top_k``.
n_candidates.title: N Candidates

version.description: Pipeline version string — encodes collection + flags. Format: ``{collection}-rerank{0|1}-parents{0|1}``. Example: ``dharma_v2-rerank0-parents1``.
version.title: Version

PipelineMetadata.title: PipelineMetadata

expand_definitional.description: Override definitional query expansion (rag-day-28). ``None`` (default) defers to the server-side setting ``glossary_expand_definitional_default``. ``True`` detects 'what is X?' / 'что такое X?' patterns and rewrites them into a longer gloss template before encode (closes the QA040 satipaṭṭhāna anomaly). ``False`` skips the rewrite for A/B debugging.
expand_definitional.title: Expand Definitional

expand_pali.description: Override Pāli-glossary query expansion (rag-day-23). ``None`` (default) defers to the server-side setting ``glossary_expand_pali_default``. ``True`` rewrites the query with the canonical Pāli lemma + its EN/RU meanings before encoding (helps bare-Pāli or cyrillic-transliterated queries). ``False`` forces a clean no-expansion run, useful for debugging an unexpected hit set.
expand_pali.title: Expand Pali

forbidden_works.description: Optional post-filter: drop hits whose ``work_canonical_id`` appears in this list. Use for per-tenant content policy (e.g. excluding texts a community considers sensitive). Filtering is post-RRF — ``top_k`` is honoured after the filter, so requests with a long forbidden list may return fewer than ``top_k`` sources.
forbidden_works.title: Forbidden Works

foundational_boost.description: Override foundational mapping boost (rag-day-28). ``None`` (default) defers to ``glossary_foundational_boost_default``. ``True`` applies a post-RRF score multiplier to canonical works of curated terms (data/glossary/foundational.yaml — e.g. dukkha → sn56.11). ``False`` disables the boost while keeping definitional expansion if enabled.
foundational_boost.title: Foundational Boost

language.description: Reserved for future filtering. Currently ignored; the corpus is English-only after rag-day-04. Accepting it now lets clients write code that survives the multi-language rollout without a contract bump.
language.title: Language

top_k.description: Number of source passages to return. Tighter cap than /api/retrieve (which allows up to 100) because /api/query is sized for direct LLM consumption — 5 parents at ~1.5K tokens each fits comfortably in any context window.
top_k.title: Top K

QueryResponse.description: Body of the response from POST /api/query.

sources.description: Top-k source passages in relevance order. May be empty when the query yields no hits or when ``forbidden_works`` filters everything out.
sources.title: Sources

QueryResponse.title: QueryResponse

Source.description:
One source passage in the response.

Mapped from :class:`src.retrieval.schemas.HybridHit`. Internal
diagnostic fields (``rrf_score``, ``per_channel_rank``,
``rerank_score``, ``rrf_rank``, ``parent_chunk_id``, ``is_parent``,
``chunk_id``) are intentionally **dropped** — clients should not
couple to retrieval internals. ``score`` is a normalised 0-1
summary suitable for UI sorting / thresholding.

score.description: Normalised relevance score in [0, 1]. Source: rerank score (sigmoid-mapped) when the reranker ran, otherwise RRF score scaled by the top hit. Comparable within a single response only — not across responses.
score.title: Score

segment_id.description: SuttaCentral segment identifier when present. Useful for deep-linking back to the source on suttacentral.net.
segment_id.title: Segment Id

snippet.description: The precise child fragment that matched the query. Use for highlighted citations in the UI; ``text`` is the broader passage. When parent expansion is off, ``snippet`` equals ``text``.
snippet.title: Snippet

text.description: Passage handed to the LLM. With server-side parent expansion (default), this is the parent chunk — a semantically-complete section, ~1024-2048 tokens.
text.title: Text

Source.title: Source

SourceDocument.description:
Full document body for the Reading Room.

Shape mirrors what a reader needs: identity (``canonical_id`` /
``title`` / ``title_pali``), provenance (``translation``), and
body (``paragraphs`` in document order). Diagnostic retrieval
fields are intentionally absent — this isn't a search response.

canonical_id.description: Stable canonical ID like ``mn10`` (echoes the path parameter).
canonical_id.title: Canonical Id

is_restricted.description: Vajrayana / tantric works flagged as requiring initiation. When ``True`` the frontend should gate the body behind a consent screen (Phase 5).
is_restricted.title: Is Restricted

paragraphs.description: Document body — parent-chunks in document order. Empty list is theoretically possible (work with no ingested instance) but caller should treat it as an error condition.
paragraphs.title: Paragraphs

title_pali.description: Pāli title with diacritics when known (e.g. ``Satipaṭṭhāna Sutta``). Useful to display alongside an English translation.
title_pali.title: Title Pali

translation.description: Metadata for the translation chosen by the server.

SourceDocument.title: SourceDocument

SourceParagraph.description: A single ordered paragraph (parent-chunk) of the document.

segment_id.description: SuttaCentral-style identifier when present (e.g. ``mn10:12.3``). Used for deep-linking from search results to the matching paragraph.
segment_id.title: Segment Id

sequence.description: Position in the document (0-based, document order).
sequence.title: Sequence

SourceParagraph.title: SourceParagraph

SourceTranslation.description: Provenance metadata for the rendered translation.

author.description: Translator's display name when known (e.g. ``Bhikkhu Sujato``). May be ``None`` for anonymous or compiler editions.
author.title: Author

language_code.description: Language code as stored in the corpus, typically ISO 639-3 (``eng``, ``rus``, ``pli``). Stub returns ``en``-style codes for fixture readability — frontend should accept either.
language_code.title: Language Code

license.description: SPDX-like license tag (``CC0``, ``CC-BY-4.0``, ``CC-BY-NC-4.0``, ``ARR``…). Always set — corpus invariant.
license.title: License

title.description: Translation-specific title, may differ from the work's canonical title (e.g. ``The Establishings of Mindfulness`` for ``mn10``).
title.title: Title

SourceTranslation.title: SourceTranslation

ThreadCard.description:
One canonical passage card in the LLM-free thread.

Mapped from a child ``HybridHit`` (no parent expansion — children
are bite-sized, ~384 tokens, exactly card-size). The Contextual
Retrieval prefix (``context_text``, generated at ingest by Haiku
3.5) gives the user a one-sentence "what this passage is about"
intro for free; falls back to ``None`` for parent chunks or rows
the contextualiser hasn't visited yet.

chunk_id.description: Stable UUID hex of the chunk. Echo back in the next request's ``excluded_chunk_ids`` to avoid re-showing.
chunk_id.title: Chunk Id

context_text.description: Contextual Retrieval prefix (rag-day-16): a 1-3 sentence narrative intro generated at ingest. Use as a header above ``text`` to orient the reader. ``None`` when the chunk predates the contextualiser run.
context_text.title: Context Text

text.description: Canonical chunk text — the actual passage from the Pāli Canon, no synthesis applied. ~200-500 words for a child chunk.
text.title: Text

translator.description: Translator slug from ``author_t`` (``sujato``, ``thanissaro``, ``sv``…). ``None`` for compiled or anonymous editions.
translator.title: Translator

ThreadCard.title: ThreadCard

excluded_chunk_ids.description: UUIDs of chunks already shown in earlier rounds. The server filters them out before truncating to ``top_k`` so each press of «Далее» surfaces fresh material. Pass back what the server returned in prior ``ThreadCard.chunk_id`` values — the client owns the dedup state, the server is stateless.
excluded_chunk_ids.title: Excluded Chunk Ids

top_k.description: Cards per round. Smaller than /api/query because each card is a self-contained passage the user reads end-to-end — 3 is the sweet spot for a single scroll-view.
top_k.title: Top K

ThreadResponse.description: Body of the response from POST /api/thread/next.

cards.description: Next batch of passages, top-scoring first. Empty list when the retrieval pool is exhausted for this query — see ``exhausted``.
cards.title: Cards

exhausted.description: ``True`` when fewer than ``top_k`` fresh passages remained after applying ``excluded_chunk_ids``. The frontend should hide the «Далее» button (or label it «End of thread»).
exhausted.title: Exhausted

ThreadResponse.title: ThreadResponse

info.description: Open, citation-first RAG system for Buddhist teachings. Phase 1 MVP: health endpoint only.
info.title: Dharma RAG

200.description: An ``text/event-stream`` connection. Each event is one of: ``retrieval_done``, ``token``, ``citation``, ``done``, ``error``. See :mod:`src.answer.stream_schemas` for payloads.

post.summary: LLM-grounded answer streamed as Server-Sent Events
Record a 👍/👎 vote on a previous answer
Stable production retrieval — top-k source passages

404.description: Work not found in the corpus.

get.summary: Full document body for the Reading Room

post.summary: LLM-free passage rotation — next batch of canonical chunks

get.description:
Return a static OK payload.

This endpoint deliberately does NOT check downstream services so
it can be used by orchestrators as a cheap liveness signal. A
deeper readiness probe will be added in a later phase.
